mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >65% of triggered instances over 20 days, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 4.5 percent signal labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >70% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5 during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real savings indicates consumption-driven demand outpacing income, which fuels equity rallies over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the GDP-savings spread and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 in post-shock recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above two percent annualized signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, supporting positive equity returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during mid-cycle expansion windows over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates turning positive compress growth stock valuations by raising the discount rate, generating negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in >60% of instances when real rates are positive during monetary tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll growth expands the consumer base and corporate revenues, driving positive quarterly equity returns across economic cycles.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns across rolling 3-year windows (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, triggering negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >70% of instances when HOUST < 1200 during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Productivity growth exceeding 1.5 percent reduces unit labor costs, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Profit margin expansion fails to materialize when productivity spread > 1.5%, resulting in negative forward returns in >65% of cases.
